{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_eb6e5c57a3ba4288b9542315","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n69\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\n70\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n71\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\n72\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n73\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\n74\t\n75\t/// Classification of indent transition types\n76\t///\n77\t/// This enum represents the three possible types of indent transitions\n78\t/// that can occur during YAML parsing.\n79\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n80\tpub enum IndentTransitionType {\n81\t    /// Indent increased, indicating entry into a deeper scope\n82\t    EnterScope,\n83\t    /// Indent decreased, indicating exit to a parent scope\n84\t    ExitScope,\n85\t    /// No indent change, staying at the same scope level\n86\t    SameLevel,\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\timpl IndentTransitionType {\n90\t    /// Classify an indent transition based on from/to indents\n91\t    pub fn classify(from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize) -> Self {\n92\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n93\t        match to_indent.cmp(&from_indent) {\n94\t            Ordering::Greater => Self::EnterScope,\n95\t            Ordering::Less => Self::ExitScope,\n96\t            Ordering::Equal => Self::SameLevel,\n97\t        }\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Check if this is an EnterScope transition\n101\t    pub fn is_enter_scope(&self) -> bool {\n102\t        matches!(self, Self::EnterScope)\n103\t    }\n104\t\n105\t    /// Check if this is an ExitScope transition\n106\t    pub fn is_exit_scope(&self) -> bool {\n107\t        matches!(self, Self::ExitScope)\n108\t    }\n109\t\n110\t    /// Check if this is a SameLevel transition\n111\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n112\t        matches!(self, Self::SameLevel)\n113\t    }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransitionType {\n117\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n118\t        match self {\n119\t            Self::EnterScope => write!(f, \"enter-scope\"),\n120\t            Self::ExitScope => write!(f, \"exit-scope\"),\n121\t            Self::SameLevel => write!(f, \"same-level\"),\n122\t        }\n123\t    }\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\t/// State machine for tracking indent level transitions\n127\t///\n128\t/// This struct maintains the current state of the parser's indent transition\n129\t/// tracking, enabling classification of scope operations during parsing.\n130\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n131\tpub struct IndentTransitionState {\n132\t    /// The previous indentation level\n133\t    pub from_indent: usize,\n134\t    /// The current indentation level\n135\t    pub to_indent: usize,\n136\t    /// The type of the last transition\n137\t    pub last_transition_type: IndentTransitionType,\n138\t    /// Whether the last transition had a key token\n139\t    pub last_had_key: bool,\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\timpl IndentTransitionState {\n143\t    /// Create a new transition state in initial state\n144\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n145\t        Self {\n146\t            from_indent: 0,\n147\t            to_indent: 0,\n148\t            last_transition_type: IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n149\t            last_had_key: false,\n150\t        }\n151\t    }\n152\t\n153\t    /// Update the state with a new indent transition\n154\t    ///\n155\t    /// # Arguments\n156\t    ///\n157\t    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n158\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n159\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n160\t    pub fn update(&mut self, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool) {\n161\t        self.from_indent = from_indent;\n162\t        self.to_indent = to_indent;\n163\t        self.last_transition_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(from_indent, to_indent);\n164\t        self.last_had_key = has_key;\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Get the current transition type\n168\t    pub fn current_transition_type(&self) -> IndentTransitionType {\n169\t        self.last_transition_type\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    /// Check if the last transition was an enter-scope\n173\t    pub fn is_entering_scope(&self) -> bool {\n174\t        self.last_transition_type.is_enter_scope()\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    /// Check if the last transition was an exit-scope\n178\t    pub fn is_exiting_scope(&self) -> bool {\n179\t        self.last_transition_type.is_exit_scope()\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Check if the last transition was same-level\n183\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n184\t        self.last_transition_type.is_same_level()\n185\t    }\n186\t\n187\t    /// Get a description of the current state\n188\t    pub fn describe(&self) -> String {\n189\t        format!(\n190\t            \"IndentTransitionState(from={}, to={}, type={}, had_key={})\",\n191\t            self.from_indent, self.to_indent, self.last_transition_type, self.last_had_key\n192\t        )\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    /// Reset to initial state\n196\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n197\t        *self = Self::new();\n198\t    }\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\timpl Default for IndentTransitionState {\n202\t    fn default() -> Self {\n203\t        Self::new()\n204\t    }\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransitionState {\n208\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n209\t        write!(\n210\t            f,\n211\t            \"IndentTransitionState(from={}, to={}, type={}, had_key={})\",\n212\t            self.from_indent, self.to_indent, self.last_transition_type, self.last_had_key\n213\t        )\n214\t    }\n215\t}\n216\t\n217\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n218\t///\n219\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n220\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n221\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n222\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n223\tpub struct Scope {\n224\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n225\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n226\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n227\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n228\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n229\t    pub start_line: usize,\n230\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n231\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n232\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n233\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n234\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n235\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n236\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n237\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\timpl Scope {\n241\t    /// Create a new scope\n242\t    ///\n243\t    /// # Arguments\n244\t    ///\n245\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n246\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n247\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n248\t    ///\n249\t    /// # Examples\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// ```\n252\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n253\t    ///\n254\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n255\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n256\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n257\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n258\t    /// ```\n259\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n260\t        Self {\n261\t            indent_level,\n262\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n263\t            start_line,\n264\t            parent_key,\n265\t            is_flow_style: false,\n266\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n267\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n268\t        }\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n272\t    ///\n273\t    /// # Arguments\n274\t    ///\n275\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n276\t    ///\n277\t    /// # Returns\n278\t    ///\n279\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n280\t    ///\n281\t    /// # Examples\n282\t    ///\n283\t    /// ```\n284\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n285\t    ///\n286\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n287\t    ///\n288\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n289\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n290\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n291\t    /// ```\n292\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n293\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n297\t    ///\n298\t    /// # Arguments\n299\t    ///\n300\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n301\t    ///\n302\t    /// # Returns\n303\t    ///\n304\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n305\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n306\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n307\t    }\n308\t\n309\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n310\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n311\t        self.keys.len()\n312\t    }\n313\t\n314\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n315\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n316\t        self.keys.clear();\n317\t    }\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n321\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n322\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n323\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n324\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n325\t        }\n326\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n327\t    }\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n331\t///\n332\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n333\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n334\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n335\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n336\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n337\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n338\t    pub scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n339\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n340\t    base_indent: usize,\n341\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n342\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n343\t    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n344\t    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n345\t    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n346\t    last_indent: usize,\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\timpl ScopeStack {\n350\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n351\t    ///\n352\t    /// # Arguments\n353\t    ///\n354\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n355\t    ///\n356\t    /// # Examples\n357\t    ///\n358\t    /// ```\n359\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n360\t    ///\n361\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n362\t    /// ```\n363\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n364\t        Self {\n365\t            scopes: Vec::new(), // Empty stack - initialized with no scopes\n366\t            base_indent,\n367\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n368\t            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\n369\t            last_indent: 0,\n370\t        }\n371\t    }\n372\t\n373\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n374\t    ///\n375\t    /// # Returns\n376\t    ///\n377\t    /// `Some(&mut Scope)` if the stack has at least one scope, `None` if empty\n378\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> Option<&mut Scope> {\n379\t        self.scopes.last_mut()\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n383\t    ///\n384\t    /// # Returns\n385\t    ///\n386\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if the stack has at least one scope, `None` if empty\n387\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> Option<&Scope> {\n388\t        self.scopes.last()\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// # Arguments\n394\t    ///\n395\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n396\t    ///\n397\t    /// # Returns\n398\t    ///\n399\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n400\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n401\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n405\t    ///\n406\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n407\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n408\t    ///\n409\t    /// # Arguments\n410\t    ///\n411\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n412\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n413\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n414\t    ///\n415\t    /// # Examples\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// ```\n418\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n419\t    ///\n420\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n421\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n422\t    /// ```\n423\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n424\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\n425\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n426\t        {\n427\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n428\t            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n429\t            log_debug!(\n430\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n431\t                scope_type,\n432\t                line,\n433\t                indent_level,\n434\t                parent_info,\n435\t                self.depth(),\n436\t                self.get_scope_path()\n437\t            );\n438\t        }\n439\t\n440\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n441\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n442\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n443\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n444\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n445\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n446\t\n447\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n448\t            let before_depth = self.depth();\n449\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n450\t\n451\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n452\t            {\n453\t                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n454\t                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n455\t                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n456\t                    .collect();\n457\t                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n458\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n459\t                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n460\t                             scopes_to_remove);\n461\t                }\n462\t            }\n463\t\n464\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n465\t\n466\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n467\t            {\n468\t                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n469\t                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n470\t                if removed_count > 0 {\n471\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n472\t                             removed_count,\n473\t                             before_path,\n474\t                             self.get_scope_path());\n475\t                }\n476\t            }\n477\t\n478\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n479\t\n480\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n481\t            {\n482\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n483\t            }\n484\t        } else {\n485\t            // Create new scope\n486\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n487\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n488\t\n489\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n490\t            {\n491\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n492\t            }\n493\t        }\n494\t\n495\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n496\t        {\n497\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n498\t        }\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    /// Exit one level to immediate parent scope\n502\t    ///\n503\t    /// This method exits from the current scope to its immediate parent scope.\n504\t    /// It's a convenience method for single-level scope exits.\n505\t    ///\n506\t    /// # Returns\n507\t    ///\n508\t    /// `true` if successfully exited to parent, `false` if already at root scope\n509\t    ///\n510\t    /// # Examples\n511\t    ///\n512\t    /// ```\n513\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n514\t    ///\n515\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n516\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n517\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n518\t    ///\n519\t    /// // Exit from child to parent\n520\t    /// let exited = stack.exit_one_level();\n521\t    /// assert!(exited);\n522\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n523\t    ///\n524\t    /// // Exit from parent to root\n525\t    /// let exited = stack.exit_one_level();\n526\t    /// assert!(exited);\n527\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// // Already at root - cannot exit further\n530\t    /// let exited = stack.exit_one_level();\n531\t    /// assert!(!exited);\n532\t    /// ```\n533\t    pub fn exit_one_level(&mut self) -> bool {\n534\t        // Edge case: already at root scope (depth of 1)\n535\t        if self.depth() <= 1 {\n536\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n537\t            {\n538\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT ONE LEVEL] Already at root scope (depth={}), cannot exit further\", self.depth());\n539\t            }\n540\t            return false;\n541\t        }\n542\t\n543\t        // Get the current scope's indent level\n544\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n545\t\n546\t        // Find the parent scope's indent level\n547\t        // The parent is the second-to-last scope in the stack\n548\t        let parent_indent = if self.depth() >= 2 {\n549\t            self.scopes[self.depth() - 2].indent_level\n550\t        } else {\n551\t            // Should not reach here due to the depth check above,\n552\t            // but handle gracefully by defaulting to root indent (0)\n553\t            0\n554\t        };\n555\t\n556\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n557\t        {\n558\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n559\t            log_debug!(\n560\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT ONE LEVEL] exiting from indent={} to parent indent={}, path='{}'\",\n561\t                current_indent,\n562\t                parent_indent,\n563\t                before_path\n564\t            );\n565\t        }\n566\t\n567\t        // Use the existing exit_to_scope method with the parent's indent\n568\t        self.exit_to_scope(parent_indent);\n569\t\n570\t        true\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n574\t    ///\n575\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n576\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n577\t    ///\n578\t    /// # Arguments\n579\t    ///\n580\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n581\t    ///\n582\t    /// # Examples\n583\t    ///\n584\t    /// ```\n585\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n586\t    ///\n587\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n588\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n589\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n590\t    /// ```\n591\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n592\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n593\t        {\n594\t            log_debug!(\n595\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n596\t                target_indent,\n597\t                self.depth(),\n598\t                self.current_indent(),\n599\t                self.get_scope_path()\n600\t            );\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n604\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n605\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n606\t            {\n607\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n608\t            }\n609\t            return;\n610\t        }\n611\t\n612\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n613\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n614\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n615\t            .count() == 0;\n616\t\n617\t        if would_be_empty {\n618\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n619\t            {\n620\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n621\t            }\n622\t            // Keep at least the root scope\n623\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n624\t            return;\n625\t        }\n626\t\n627\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n628\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n629\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n630\t\n631\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n632\t        {\n633\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n634\t        }\n635\t\n636\t        // Search for target scope in hierarchy\n637\t        // This handles cases where the exact target indent doesn't exist in the stack\n638\t        // by finding the closest parent scope or creating a fallback if needed\n639\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n640\t            // No exact match at target indent - search for closest parent\n641\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n642\t            {\n643\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at target_indent={}, searching for closest parent scope\",\n644\t                         target_indent);\n645\t            }\n646\t\n647\t            // Find the closest scope with indent <= target_indent\n648\t            let closest_scope = self.scopes.iter()\n649\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n650\t                .max_by_key(|s| s.indent_level);\n651\t\n652\t            match closest_scope {\n653\t                Some(scope) => {\n654\t                    // Found a parent scope - use that instead\n655\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n656\t                    {\n657\t                        log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found closest parent scope at indent={}, path='{}', using existing scope\",\n658\t                                 scope.indent_level, self.get_scope_path());\n659\t                    }\n660\t                    // We've already retained scopes <= target_indent, so we're at the closest scope\n661\t                }\n662\t                None => {\n663\t                    // No suitable parent scope found - this shouldn't happen in normal YAML\n664\t                    // but we handle it gracefully by creating a fallback scope\n665\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n666\t                    {\n667\t                        log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No suitable parent scope found, creating fallback scope at indent={}\",\n668\t                                 target_indent);\n669\t                    }\n670\t                    let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n671\t                    self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n672\t                }\n673\t            }\n674\t        }\n675\t\n676\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n677\t        {\n678\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n679\t        }\n680\t    }\n681\t\n682\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n683\t    ///\n684\t    /// # Arguments\n685\t    ///\n686\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n687\t    ///\n688\t    /// # Returns\n689\t    ///\n690\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n691\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n692\t        self.scopes.last()\n693\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n694\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n698\t    ///\n699\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n700\t    ///\n701\t    /// # Arguments\n702\t    ///\n703\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n704\t    ///\n705\t    /// # Returns\n706\t    ///\n707\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n708\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n709\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n710\t    }\n711\t\n712\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n713\t    ///\n714\t    /// # Arguments\n715\t    ///\n716\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n717\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n718\t    ///\n719\t    /// # Returns\n720\t    ///\n721\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n722\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n723\t    ///\n724\t    /// # Examples\n725\t    ///\n726\t    /// ```\n727\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n728\t    ///\n729\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n730\t    ///\n731\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n732\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n733\t    ///\n734\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n735\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n736\t    /// ```\n737\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n738\t        // Auto-create root scope if stack is empty\n739\t        if self.scopes.is_empty() {\n740\t            self.scopes.push(Scope::new(0, 0, None));\n741\t        }\n742\t\n743\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n744\t\n745\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n746\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n747\t            let scope = self.current_scope().unwrap();\n748\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n749\t                key: key.to_string(),\n750\t                scope_path,\n751\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n752\t                duplicate_line: line,\n753\t            })\n754\t        } else {\n755\t            let scope = self.current_scope().unwrap();\n756\t            scope.add_key(key);\n757\t            Ok(())\n758\t        }\n759\t    }\n760\t\n761\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n762\t    ///\n763\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n764\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n765\t    ///\n766\t    /// # Returns\n767\t    ///\n768\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n769\t    ///\n770\t    /// # Examples\n771\t    ///\n772\t    /// ```\n773\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n774\t    ///\n775\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n776\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n777\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n778\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n779\t    /// ```\n780\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n781\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n782\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n783\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n784\t                path.push(key.clone());\n785\t            }\n786\t        }\n787\t        path.join(\".\")\n788\t    }\n789\t\n790\t    /// Get current indent level\n791\t    ///\n792\t    /// # Returns\n793\t    ///\n794\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope, or 0 if stack is empty\n795\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n796\t        self.scopes.last()\n797\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n798\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n799\t    }\n800\t\n801\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n802\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n803\t        self.scopes.len()\n804\t    }\n805\t\n806\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to empty\n807\t    ///\n808\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with no scopes.\n809\t    /// The next add_key() will auto-create a root scope.\n810\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n811\t        self.scopes.clear();\n812\t        self.clear_indent_transitions();\n813\t    }\n814\t\n815\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n816\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n817\t        self.base_indent\n818\t    }\n819\t\n820\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n821\t    ///\n822\t    /// # Arguments\n823\t    ///\n824\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n825\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n826\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n827\t    }\n828\t\n829\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n830\t    ///\n831\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n832\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n833\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n834\t    ///\n835\t    /// # Arguments\n836\t    ///\n837\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n838\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n839\t    ///\n840\t    /// # Examples\n841\t    ///\n842\t    /// ```\n843\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n844\t    ///\n845\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n846\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n847\t    /// ```\n848\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n849\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n850\t        {\n851\t            log_debug!(\n852\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n853\t                line,\n854\t                indent_level,\n855\t                self.depth(),\n856\t                self.get_scope_path()\n857\t            );\n858\t        }\n859\t\n860\t        // Remove all scopes at or deeper than this level\n861\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we clear any existing scope at this level\n862\t        // (including parent mappings) and all deeper scopes, then add a new sequence scope\n863\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n864\t        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n865\t\n866\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n867\t        {\n868\t            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n869\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level)\n870\t                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n871\t                .collect();\n872\t            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n873\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} scopes at or deeper than indent={}: {:?}\",\n874\t                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n875\t                         indent_level,\n876\t                         scopes_to_remove);\n877\t            }\n878\t        }\n879\t\n880\t        // Only retain scopes shallower than indent_level\n881\t        // Sequence scopes completely replace any mapping at the same level\n882\t        // (unlike sibling mappings which preserve the parent)\n883\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n884\t\n885\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n886\t        {\n887\t            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n888\t            if removed_count > 0 {\n889\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n890\t                         removed_count,\n891\t                         before_path,\n892\t                         self.get_scope_path());\n893\t            }\n894\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes at or deeper than this level (replaces any parent mapping)\", indent_level);\n895\t        }\n896\t\n897\t        // Always create a new scope for each sequence item\n898\t        // Each sequence item needs a fresh scope - we don't reuse existing sequence scopes\n899\t        let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n900\t        new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n901\t        self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n902\t        new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n903\t        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n904\t\n905\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n906\t        {\n907\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n908\t        }\n909\t\n910\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n911\t        {\n912\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n913\t        }\n914\t    }\n915\t\n916\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n917\t    ///\n918\t    /// # Returns\n919\t    ///\n920\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n921\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n922\t        self.scopes.last()\n923\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n924\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n925\t    }\n926\t\n927\t    /// Record an indent transition\n928\t    ///\n929\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n930\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n931\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n932\t    ///\n933\t    /// # Arguments\n934\t    ///\n935\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n936\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n937\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n938\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n939\t    ///\n940\t    /// Record an indent transition\n941\t    ///\n942\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n943\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n944\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n945\t    ///\n946\t    /// # Arguments\n947\t    ///\n948\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n949\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n950\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n951\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n952\t    ///\n953\t    /// # Examples\n954\t    ///\n955\t    /// ```\n956\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n957\t    ///\n958\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n959\t    /// stack.record_indent_transition(5, 2, false, \"  # blank line with indent\");\n960\t    /// ```\n961\t    pub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\n962\t        // Only record if the indent actually changed\n963\t        if new_indent != self.last_indent {\n964\t            // Store the old indent before updating\n965\t            let old_indent = self.last_indent;\n966\t\n967\t            // Classify the line type\n968\t            let line_classification = classify_line_type(raw_line);\n969\t\n970\t            let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n971\t                line_number,\n972\t                old_indent,\n973\t                new_indent,\n974\t                has_key,\n975\t                raw_line,\n976\t                line_classification,\n977\t            );\n978\t            self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\n979\t            self.last_indent = new_indent;\n980\t\n981\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n982\t            {\n983\t                let direction = if new_indent > old_indent {\n984\t                    \"increase\"\n985\t                } else {\n986\t                    \"decrease\"\n987\t                };\n988\t                let key_status = if has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n989\t                log_debug!(\n990\t                    \"[INDENT TRANSITION] line={}, {}→{}, {}, {}, raw='{}'\",\n991\t                    line_number,\n992\t                    old_indent,\n993\t                    new_indent,\n994\t                    key_status,\n995\t                    line_classification,\n996\t                    raw_line.trim()\n997\t                );\n998\t            }\n999\t        }\n1000\t    }\n1001\t\n1002\t    /// Get all recorded indent transitions\n1003\t    ///\n1004\t    /// # Returns\n1005\t    ///\n1006\t    /// A slice of all indent transitions recorded during parsing\n1007\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self) -> &[IndentTransition] {\n1008\t        &self.indent_transitions\n1009\t    }\n1010\t\n1011\t    /// Get indent transitions without keys\n1012\t    ///\n1013\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines without key tokens,\n1014\t    /// such as blank lines or comments.\n1015\t    ///\n1016\t    /// # Returns\n1017\t    ///\n1018\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred without keys\n1019\t    pub fn get_transitions_without_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1020\t        self.indent_transitions\n1021\t            .iter()\n1022\t            .filter(|t| t.is_without_key())\n1023\t            .collect()\n1024\t    }\n1025\t\n1026\t    /// Get indent transitions with keys\n1027\t    ///\n1028\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines with key tokens.\n1029\t    ///\n1030\t    /// # Returns\n1031\t    ///\n1032\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred with keys\n1033\t    pub fn get_transitions_with_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1034\t        self.indent_transitions\n1035\t            .iter()\n1036\t            .filter(|t| t.has_key)\n1037\t            .collect()\n1038\t    }\n1039\t\n1040\t    /// Get enter-scope transitions\n1041\t    ///\n1042\t    /// Returns only those transitions that represent entering a deeper scope.\n1043\t    ///\n1044\t    /// # Returns\n1045\t    ///\n1046\t    /// A vector of enter-scope transitions\n1047\t    pub fn get_enter_scope_transitions(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1048\t        self.indent_transitions\n1049\t            .iter()\n1050\t            .filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope())\n1051\t            .collect()\n1052\t    }\n1053\t\n1054\t    /// Get exit-scope transitions\n1055\t    ///\n1056\t    /// Returns only those transitions that represent exiting to a parent scope.\n1057\t    ///\n1058\t    /// # Returns\n1059\t    ///\n1060\t    /// A vector of exit-scope transitions\n1061\t    pub fn get_exit_scope_transitions(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1062\t        self.indent_transitions\n1063\t            .iter()\n1064\t            .filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope())\n1065\t            .collect()\n1066\t    }\n1067\t\n1068\t    /// Get same-level transitions\n1069\t    ///\n1070\t    /// Returns only those transitions that represent staying at the same level.\n1071\t    ///\n1072\t    /// # Returns\n1073\t    ///\n1074\t    /// A vector of same-level transitions\n1075\t    pub fn get_same_level_transitions(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1076\t        self.indent_transitions\n1077\t            .iter()\n1078\t            .filter(|t| t.is_same_level())\n1079\t            .collect()\n1080\t    }\n1081\t\n1082\t    /// Get transition count by type\n1083\t    ///\n1084\t    /// Returns a tuple of (enter_count, exit_count, same_level_count).\n1085\t    ///\n1086\t    /// # Returns\n1087\t    ///\n1088\t    /// A tuple with counts of each transition type\n1089\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&self) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n1090\t        let enter = self.get_enter_scope_transitions().len();\n1091\t        let exit = self.get_exit_scope_transitions().len();\n1092\t        let same = self.get_same_level_transitions().len();\n1093\t        (enter, exit, same)\n1094\t    }\n1095\t\n1096\t    /// Clear all indent transitions\n1097\t    ///\n1098\t    /// This is typically called when resetting the scope stack for a new document.\n1099\t    pub fn clear_indent_transitions(&mut self) {\n1100\t        self.indent_transitions.clear();\n1101\t        self.last_indent = 0;\n1102\t    }\n1103\t\n1104\t    /// Get the last recorded indent level\n1105\t    ///\n1106\t    /// # Returns\n1107\t    ///\n1108\t    /// The most recent indent level recorded\n1109\t    pub fn get_last_indent(&self) -> usize {\n1110\t        self.last_indent\n1111\t    }\n1112\t\n1113\t    /// Set the last indent level directly\n1114\t    ///\n1115\t    /// This can be used to initialize the indent tracking or to correct it after\n1116\t    /// document markers or other special constructs.\n1117\t    ///\n1118\t    /// # Arguments\n1119\t    ///\n1120\t    /// * `indent` - The indent level to set\n1121\t    pub fn set_last_indent(&mut self, indent: usize) {\n1122\t        self.last_indent = indent;\n1123\t    }\n1124\t\n1125\t    /// Process an indent transition without a key token\n1126\t    ///\n1127\t    /// This method handles indentation changes that occur on lines without key tokens,\n1128\t    /// such as blank lines or comments. It determines if the indent change represents\n1129\t    /// a valid scope transition and triggers the appropriate scope entry/exit.\n1130\t    ///\n1131\t    /// # Arguments\n1132\t    ///\n1133\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1134\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n1135\t    ///\n1136\t    /// # Returns\n1137\t    ///\n1138\t    /// `true` if a scope transition occurred, `false` otherwise\n1139\t    ///\n1140\t    /// # Examples\n1141\t    ///\n1142\t    /// ```\n1143\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n1144\t    ///\n1145\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n1146\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n1147\t    /// // Process indent decrease on blank line\n1148\t    /// let transitioned = stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(5, 0);\n1149\t    /// assert!(transitioned); // Should exit to root scope\n1150\t    /// ```\n1151\t    pub fn process_indent_transition_without_key(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize) -> bool {\n1152\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n1153\t\n1154\t        // Compare new indent with current scope indent\n1155\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n1156\t\n1157\t        match new_indent.cmp(&current_indent) {\n1158\t            Ordering::Greater => {\n1159\t                // Indent increased - but without a key, this is unusual\n1160\t                // We don't enter a new scope without a parent key\n1161\t                // Just record the transition for tracking purposes\n1162\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n1163\t                {\n1164\t                    log_debug!(\n1165\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (increase), NO SCOPE ENTRY (no parent key)\",\n1166\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent\n1167\t                    );\n1168\t                }\n1169\t                false\n1170\t            }\n1171\t            Ordering::Less => {\n1172\t                // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n1173\t                // This is valid even without a key (e.g., blank line at end of nested block)\n1174\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n1175\t                {\n1176\t                    let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n1177\t                    log_debug!(\n1178\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (decrease), exiting scope: '{}'\",\n1179\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent, before_path\n1180\t                    );\n1181\t                }\n1182\t\n1183\t                self.exit_to_scope(new_indent);\n1184\t                true\n1185\t            }\n1186\t            Ordering::Equal => {\n1187\t                // Same indent - no scope change needed\n1188\t                false\n1189\t            }\n1190\t        }\n1191\t    }\n1192\t}\n1193\t\n1194\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n1195\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1196\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n1197\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n1198\t    }\n1199\t}\n1200\t\n1201\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n1202\t///\n1203\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n1204\t/// in the current scope.\n1205\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n1206\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n1207\t    /// The duplicate key\n1208\t    pub key: String,\n1209\t    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n1210\t    pub scope_path: String,\n1211\t    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n1212\t    pub first_line: usize,\n1213\t    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n1214\t    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n1215\t}\n1216\t\n1217\timpl DuplicateKeyError {\n1218\t    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n1219\t    ///\n1220\t    /// # Arguments\n1221\t    ///\n1222\t    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n1223\t    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n1224\t    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n1225\t    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n1226\t    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n1227\t        Self {\n1228\t            key,\n1229\t            scope_path,\n1230\t            first_line,\n1231\t            duplicate_line,\n1232\t        }\n1233\t    }\n1234\t\n1235\t    /// Get a formatted error message\n1236\t    ///\n1237\t    /// # Returns\n1238\t    ///\n1239\t    /// A human-readable error message\n1240\t    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n1241\t        format!(\n1242\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n1243\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n1244\t        )\n1245\t    }\n1246\t}\n1247\t\n1248\timpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n1249\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1250\t        write!(\n1251\t            f,\n1252\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n1253\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n1254\t        )\n1255\t    }\n1256\t}\n1257\t\n1258\timpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n1259\t\n1260\t/// Key context classification\n1261\t///\n1262\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n1263\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n1264\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n1265\tpub enum KeyContext {\n1266\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n1267\t    ///\n1268\t    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n1269\t    InlineScalar {\n1270\t        /// The key name\n1271\t        key: String,\n1272\t        /// The scalar value\n1273\t        value: String,\n1274\t    },\n1275\t\n1276\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n1277\t    ///\n1278\t    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n1279\t    ParentMapping {\n1280\t        /// The parent key name\n1281\t        key: String,\n1282\t    },\n1283\t\n1284\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n1285\t    ///\n1286\t    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n1287\t    ParentSequence {\n1288\t        /// The parent key name\n1289\t        key: String,\n1290\t    },\n1291\t}\n1292\t\n1293\timpl KeyContext {\n1294\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n1295\t    ///\n1296\t    /// # Returns\n1297\t    ///\n1298\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n1299\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n1300\t        match self {\n1301\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n1302\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n1303\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n1304\t        }\n1305\t    }\n1306\t\n1307\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n1308\t    ///\n1309\t    /// # Returns\n1310\t    ///\n1311\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n1312\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n1313\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n1314\t    }\n1315\t\n1316\t    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n1317\t    ///\n1318\t    /// # Returns\n1319\t    ///\n1320\t    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n1321\t    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n1322\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n1323\t    }\n1324\t}\n1325\t\n1326\timpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n1327\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1328\t        match self {\n1329\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n1330\t                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n1331\t            }\n1332\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n1333\t                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n1334\t            }\n1335\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n1336\t                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n1337\t            }\n1338\t        }\n1339\t    }\n1340\t}\n1341\t\n1342\t/// Extract key context from a line\n1343\t///\n1344\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n1345\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n1346\t///\n1347\t/// # Arguments\n1348\t///\n1349\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n1350\t///\n1351\t/// # Returns\n1352\t///\n1353\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n1354\t///\n1355\t/// # Examples\n1356\t///\n1357\t/// ```\n1358\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n1359\t///\n1360\t/// // Inline scalar\n1361\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n1362\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n1363\t///\n1364\t/// // Parent mapping\n1365\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n1366\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n1367\t/// ```\n1368\tpub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n1369\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n1370\t\n1371\t    // Find colon position\n1372\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n1373\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n1374\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n1375\t\n1376\t    // Skip if key is empty\n1377\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n1378\t    if key.is_empty() {\n1379\t        return None;\n1380\t    }\n1381\t\n1382\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n1383\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n1384\t        return None;\n1385\t    }\n1386\t\n1387\t    // Strip sequence dash from key if present\n1388\t    // Handles: \"- name: value\" -> key should be \"name\", not \"- name\"\n1389\t    let actual_key = if key.starts_with(\"- \") {\n1390\t        // Remove the \"- \" prefix (dash and space)\n1391\t        key[2..].trim()\n1392\t    } else if key.starts_with('-') && key.len() > 1 {\n1393\t        // Dash followed by non-space (invalid, but handle gracefully)\n1394\t        &key[1..]\n1395\t    } else {\n1396\t        key\n1397\t    };\n1398\t\n1399\t    // Skip if actual key is empty after stripping dash\n1400\t    if actual_key.is_empty() {\n1401\t        return None;\n1402\t    }\n1403\t\n1404\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n1405\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n1406\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: actual_key.to_string() }\n1407\t    } else {\n1408\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n1409\t            key: actual_key.to_string(),\n1410\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n1411\t        }\n1412\t    };\n1413\t\n1414\t    Some(context)\n1415\t}\n1416\t\n1417\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n1418\t///\n1419\t/// # Arguments\n1420\t///\n1421\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n1422\t///\n1423\t/// # Returns\n1424\t///\n1425\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n1426\t///\n1427\t/// # Examples\n1428\t///\n1429\t/// ```\n1430\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n1431\t///\n1432\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n1433\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n1434\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n1435\t/// ```\n1436\tpub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n1437\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n1438\t}\n1439\t\n1440\t/// Classify a YAML line as key-bearing or indent-only\n1441\t///\n1442\t/// This function analyzes a line to determine if it contains a key token,\n1443\t/// which is essential for proper scope transition handling.\n1444\t///\n1445\t/// # Arguments\n1446\t///\n1447\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to classify\n1448\t///\n1449\t/// # Returns\n1450\t///\n1451\t/// The `LineClassification` for the line\n1452\t///\n1453\t/// # Examples\n1454\t///\n1455\t/// ```\n1456\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::classify_line_type;\n1457\t///\n1458\t/// // Key-bearing lines\n1459\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"key: value\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::KeyBearing));\n1460\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"  nested:\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::KeyBearing));\n1461\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"- item\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::KeyBearing));\n1462\t///\n1463\t/// // Indent-only lines (no key token)\n1464\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"  some value\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::IndentOnly));\n1465\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"    more text\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::IndentOnly));\n1466\t///\n1467\t/// // Empty lines\n1468\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::Empty));\n1469\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"    \"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::Empty));\n1470\t/// ```\n1471\tpub fn classify_line_type(line: &str) -> LineClassification {\n1472\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n1473\t\n1474\t    // Empty or whitespace-only lines\n1475\t    if trimmed.is_empty() {\n1476\t        return LineClassification::Empty;\n1477\t    }\n1478\t\n1479\t    // Check if line has a key token by using existing extract_key_context\n1480\t    // This handles all YAML key patterns including:\n1481\t    // - Simple keys: \"key: value\"\n1482\t    // - Parent keys: \"key:\"\n1483\t    // - Sequence items with keys: \"- key: value\"\n1484\t    // - Nested keys with proper indentation\n1485\t    if extract_key_context(line).is_some() {\n1486\t        LineClassification::KeyBearing\n1487\t    } else {\n1488\t        LineClassification::IndentOnly\n1489\t    }\n1490\t}\n1491\t\n1492\t/// Check if a line contains key tokens\n1493\t///\n1494\t/// This is a convenience function that returns true if the line is key-bearing.\n1495\t///\n1496\t/// # Arguments\n1497\t///\n1498\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to check\n1499\t///\n1500\t/// # Returns\n1501\t///\n1502\t/// `true` if the line contains key tokens, `false` otherwise\n1503\t///\n1504\t/// # Examples\n1505\t///\n1506\t/// ```\n1507\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::has_key_token;\n1508\t///\n1509\t/// assert!(has_key_token(\"key: value\"));\n1510\t/// assert!(has_key_token(\"  nested: value\"));\n1511\t/// assert!(!has_key_token(\"  some text\"));\n1512\t/// assert!(!has_key_token(\"\"));\n1513\t/// ```\n1514\tpub fn has_key_token(line: &str) -> bool {\n1515\t    classify_line_type(line).is_key_bearing()\n1516\t}\n1517\t\n1518\t/// Line classification for YAML parsing\n1519\t///\n1520\t/// This enum categorizes lines based on whether they contain key tokens,\n1521\t/// which is essential for proper scope transition handling.\n1522\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n1523\tpub enum LineClassification {\n1524\t    /// Key-bearing line (contains a YAML key token like \"key:\", \"- key:\", etc.)\n1525\t    KeyBearing,\n1526\t\n1527\t    /// Indent-only line (no key token, only whitespace/content)\n1528\t    IndentOnly,\n1529\t\n1530\t    /// Empty or whitespace-only line\n1531\t    Empty,\n1532\t}\n1533\t\n1534\timpl LineClassification {\n1535\t    /// Check if this line type is key-bearing\n1536\t    pub fn is_key_bearing(&self) -> bool {\n1537\t        matches!(self, Self::KeyBearing)\n1538\t    }\n1539\t\n1540\t    /// Check if this line type is indent-only\n1541\t    pub fn is_indent_only(&self) -> bool {\n1542\t        matches!(self, Self::IndentOnly)\n1543\t    }\n1544\t\n1545\t    /// Check if this line type is empty\n1546\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n1547\t        matches!(self, Self::Empty)\n1548\t    }\n1549\t}\n1550\t\n1551\timpl fmt::Display for LineClassification {\n1552\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1553\t        match self {\n1554\t            Self::KeyBearing => write!(f, \"key-bearing\"),\n1555\t            Self::IndentOnly => write!(f, \"indent-only\"),\n1556\t            Self::Empty => write!(f, \"empty\"),\n1557\t        }\n1558\t    }\n1559\t}\n1560\t\n1561\t/// Indent transition event recorded during parsing\n1562\t///\n1563\t/// This struct captures information about indentation level changes that occur\n1564\t/// during YAML parsing, whether or not a key token is present.\n1565\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n1566\tpub struct IndentTransition {\n1567\t    /// The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1568\t    pub line_number: usize,\n1569\t    /// The previous indentation level\n1570\t    pub from_indent: usize,\n1571\t    /// The new indentation level\n1572\t    pub to_indent: usize,\n1573\t    /// Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n1574\t    pub has_key: bool,\n1575\t    /// The raw line content (for debugging)\n1576\t    pub raw_line: String,\n1577\t    /// Line classification (key-bearing vs indent-only)\n1578\t    pub line_classification: LineClassification,\n1579\t    /// Classification of the transition type (enter-scope, exit-scope, same-level)\n1580\t    pub transition_type: IndentTransitionType,\n1581\t}\n1582\t\n1583\timpl IndentTransition {\n1584\t    /// Create a new indent transition record\n1585\t    ///\n1586\t    /// # Arguments\n1587\t    ///\n1588\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1589\t    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n1590\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n1591\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n1592\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content\n1593\t    /// * `line_classification` - Classification of the line type\n1594\t    pub fn new(line_number: usize, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str, line_classification: LineClassification) -> Self {\n1595\t        let transition_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(from_indent, to_indent);\n1596\t        Self {\n1597\t            line_number,\n1598\t            from_indent,\n1599\t            to_indent,\n1600\t            has_key,\n1601\t            raw_line: raw_line.to_string(),\n1602\t            line_classification,\n1603\t            transition_type,\n1604\t        }\n1605\t    }\n1606\t\n1607\t    /// Check if this is an indent increase\n1608\t    pub fn is_increase(&self) -> bool {\n1609\t        self.to_indent > self.from_indent\n1610\t    }\n1611\t\n1612\t    /// Check if this is an indent decrease\n1613\t    pub fn is_decrease(&self) -> bool {\n1614\t        self.to_indent < self.from_indent\n1615\t    }\n1616\t\n1617\t    /// Get the indent change amount (positive for increase, negative for decrease)\n1618\t    pub fn change_amount(&self) -> isize {\n1619\t        self.to_indent as isize - self.from_indent as isize\n1620\t    }\n1621\t\n1622\t    /// Check if this transition occurred without a key\n1623\t    pub fn is_without_key(&self) -> bool {\n1624\t        !self.has_key\n1625\t    }\n1626\t\n1627\t    /// Get the line classification for this transition\n1628\t    pub fn line_classification(&self) -> LineClassification {\n1629\t        self.line_classification\n1630\t    }\n1631\t\n1632\t    /// Get the transition type (enter-scope, exit-scope, same-level)\n1633\t    pub fn transition_type(&self) -> IndentTransitionType {\n1634\t        self.transition_type\n1635\t    }\n1636\t\n1637\t    /// Check if this is an enter-scope transition\n1638\t    pub fn is_enter_scope(&self) -> bool {\n1639\t        self.transition_type.is_enter_scope()\n1640\t    }\n1641\t\n1642\t    /// Check if this is an exit-scope transition\n1643\t    pub fn is_exit_scope(&self) -> bool {\n1644\t        self.transition_type.is_exit_scope()\n1645\t    }\n1646\t\n1647\t    /// Check if this is a same-level transition\n1648\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n1649\t        self.transition_type.is_same_level()\n1650\t    }\n1651\t\n1652\t    /// Map this indent transition to a scope operation description\n1653\t    ///\n1654\t    /// Returns a human-readable description of what scope operation\n1655\t    /// this transition represents.\n1656\t    pub fn scope_operation(&self) -> &'static str {\n1657\t        match self.transition_type {\n1658\t            IndentTransitionType::EnterScope => \"enter-scope\",\n1659\t            IndentTransitionType::ExitScope => \"exit-scope\",\n1660\t            IndentTransitionType::SameLevel => \"stay-in-scope\",\n1661\t        }\n1662\t    }\n1663\t}\n1664\t\n1665\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransition {\n1666\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1667\t        let direction = if self.is_increase() {\n1668\t            \"increase\"\n1669\t        } else if self.is_decrease() {\n1670\t            \"decrease\"\n1671\t        } else {\n1672\t            \"no-change\"\n1673\t        };\n1674\t        let key_status = if self.has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n1675\t        write!(\n1676\t            f,\n1677\t            \"IndentTransition(line={}, {}→{}, {}, {}, {}, type={})\",\n1678\t            self.line_number, self.from_indent, self.to_indent, direction, key_status, self.line_classification, self.transition_type\n1679\t        )\n1680\t    }\n1681\t}\n1682\t\n1683\t// Tests for indent transition tracking\n1684\t#[cfg(test)]\n1685\tmod tests {\n1686\t    use super::*;\n1687\t\n1688\t    #[test]\n1689\t    fn test_indent_transition_type_classify() {\n1690\t        // Test enter-scope (indent increase)\n1691\t        let trans_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(0, 2);\n1692\t        assert_eq!(trans_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope);\n1693\t        assert!(trans_type.is_enter_scope());\n1694\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_exit_scope());\n1695\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_same_level());\n1696\t\n1697\t        // Test exit-scope (indent decrease)\n1698\t        let trans_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(4, 2);\n1699\t        assert_eq!(trans_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope);\n1700\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_enter_scope());\n1701\t        assert!(trans_type.is_exit_scope());\n1702\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_same_level());\n1703\t\n1704\t        // Test same-level (no indent change)\n1705\t        let trans_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(2, 2);\n1706\t        assert_eq!(trans_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel);\n1707\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_enter_scope());\n1708\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_exit_scope());\n1709\t        assert!(trans_type.is_same_level());\n1710\t    }\n1711\t\n1712\t    #[test]\n1713\t    fn test_indent_transition_type_display() {\n1714\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", IndentTransitionType::EnterScope), \"enter-scope\");\n1715\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", IndentTransitionType::ExitScope), \"exit-scope\");\n1716\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", IndentTransitionType::SameLevel), \"same-level\");\n1717\t    }\n1718\t\n1719\t    #[test]\n1720\t    fn test_indent_transition_state() {\n1721\t        let mut state = IndentTransitionState::new();\n1722\t\n1723\t        // Initial state\n1724\t        assert_eq!(state.from_indent, 0);\n1725\t        assert_eq!(state.to_indent, 0);\n1726\t        assert_eq!(state.current_transition_type(), IndentTransitionType::SameLevel);\n1727\t        assert!(!state.last_had_key);\n1728\t\n1729\t        // Update with enter-scope transition\n1730\t        state.update(0, 2, true);\n1731\t        assert_eq!(state.from_indent, 0);\n1732\t        assert_eq!(state.to_indent, 2);\n1733\t        assert!(state.is_entering_scope());\n1734\t        assert!(!state.is_exiting_scope());\n1735\t        assert!(!state.is_same_level());\n1736\t        assert!(state.last_had_key);\n1737\t\n1738\t        // Update with exit-scope transition\n1739\t        state.update(2, 0, false);\n1740\t        assert_eq!(state.from_indent, 2);\n1741\t        assert_eq!(state.to_indent, 0);\n1742\t        assert!(!state.is_entering_scope());\n1743\t        assert!(state.is_exiting_scope());\n1744\t        assert!(!state.is_same_level());\n1745\t        assert!(!state.last_had_key);\n1746\t\n1747\t        // Update with same-level transition\n1748\t        state.update(0, 0, true);\n1749\t        assert!(state.is_same_level());\n1750\t    }\n1751\t\n1752\t    #[test]\n1753\t    fn test_indent_transition_state_describe() {\n1754\t        let mut state = IndentTransitionState::new();\n1755\t        state.update(0, 2, true);\n1756\t\n1757\t        let description = state.describe();\n1758\t        assert!(description.contains(\"from=0\"));\n1759\t        assert!(description.contains(\"to=2\"));\n1760\t        assert!(description.contains(\"enter-scope\"));\n1761\t        assert!(description.contains(\"had_key=true\"));\n1762\t\n1763\t        let display = format!(\"{}\", state);\n1764\t        assert!(display.contains(\"IndentTransitionState\"));\n1765\t    }\n1766\t\n1767\t    #[test]\n1768\t    fn test_indent_transition_state_reset() {\n1769\t        let mut state = IndentTransitionState::new();\n1770\t        state.update(0, 4, true);\n1771\t        state.reset();\n1772\t\n1773\t        assert_eq!(state.from_indent, 0);\n1774\t        assert_eq!(state.to_indent, 0);\n1775\t        assert_eq!(state.current_transition_type(), IndentTransitionType::SameLevel);\n1776\t        assert!(!state.last_had_key);\n1777\t    }\n1778\t\n1779\t    #[test]\n1780\t    fn test_indent_transition_with_type() {\n1781\t        let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n1782\t            5,\n1783\t            0,\n1784\t            2,\n1785\t            true,\n1786\t            \"key: value\",\n1787\t            LineClassification::KeyBearing,\n1788\t        );\n1789\t\n1790\t        assert_eq!(transition.line_number, 5);\n1791\t        assert_eq!(transition.from_indent, 0);\n1792\t        assert_eq!(transition.to_indent, 2);\n1793\t        assert!(transition.has_key);\n1794\t        assert!(transition.is_increase());\n1795\t        assert!(!transition.is_decrease());\n1796\t        assert!(transition.is_enter_scope());\n1797\t        assert!(!transition.is_exit_scope());\n1798\t        assert!(transition.is_without_key() == false);\n1799\t        assert_eq!(transition.transition_type(), IndentTransitionType::EnterScope);\n1800\t        assert_eq!(transition.scope_operation(), \"enter-scope\");\n1801\t    }\n1802\t\n1803\t    #[test]\n1804\t    fn test_indent_transition_exit_scope() {\n1805\t        let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n1806\t            10,\n1807\t \");\n1870\t\n1871\t        let transitions = stack.get_indent_transitions();\n1872\t        assert_eq!(transitions.len(), 3);\n1873\t\n1874\t        // Check first transition (enter-scope)\n1875\t {\n1889\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n1890\t\n1891\t        // Record transitions\n1892\t        stack.record_indent_transition(1, 2, true, \"  key: value\");\n1893\t
